name: Generate blog drafts with Notion and WordPress with instant alerts

options.systemMessage: You are the coordinator for a captions pipeline. Break the request into sub-tasks, delegate each to the matching specialist tool, then merge results into one coherent answer. Never call the same specialist twice for one request.

parameters.description: Delegate research sub-tasks for a draft to the Research specialist workflow.
Delegate draft sub-tasks for a draft to the Draft specialist workflow.

parameters.content:
### Weekly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — weekly. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Frame the Assignment

### Frame the Assignment

Shapes the item: writes `assignment`, `deadline`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Coordinator Agent

### Coordinator Agent

The AI brain: reads the input, decides which attached tools to call (and in what order), then composes the answer.

Next: Unpack Sections

### Groq Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `llama-3.3-70b-versatile`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Coordinator Agent** as `languageModel`

🔑 Groq API key

### Research Specialist (Sub-workflow)

Specialist sub-workflow exposed as a callable tool — one narrow capability per tool.

Feeds **Coordinator Agent** as `tool`

### Draft Specialist (Sub-workflow)

Specialist sub-workflow exposed as a callable tool — one narrow capability per tool.

Feeds **Coordinator Agent** as `tool`

### Enforce Result Schema

Forces the agent's answer into the JSON schema below — downstream nodes rely on this exact shape.

Feeds **Coordinator Agent** as `outputParser`

### Unpack Sections

Explodes the `output.sections` array into one item per element.

Next: Persist Deliverable to Notion

### Persist Deliverable to Notion

notion — `create` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Notion integration token

# Generate blog drafts with Notion and WordPress with instant alerts

**Pattern:** Content Generation · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Generate blog drafts with Notion and WordPress with instant alerts. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual captions is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Weekly Schedule → Frame the Assignment → Coordinator Agent → Unpack Sections → Persist Deliverable to Notion

## Use it for
- Teams that want to content generation without manual effort
- Anyone automating captions in content creation
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Responds in seconds
- Removes copy-paste errors
- Standardizes the process every time

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Groq API key; Notion integration token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your captions process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
